titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 30 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender (1876-1960) to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). This letter mainly consists of Wilfrid's declarations of affection for Lillian, as he alludes to the 'pink paw marks' he looks forward to finding on his letters. This refers to Wilfrid's pet-name for Lillian, 'P.R.', which appears to stand for 'Pink Rabbit'. His own pet-name is 'B.W.', or 'Brown Wolf'. Wilfrid also takes pleasure in the idea of the censor reading his words to Lillian. He finishes the letter with the news that Germany is willing to give up Alsace-Lorraine and an indemnity to France as part of a possible peace, but states that he is not optimistic.Sir Wilfrid Spender (1876 - 1960) was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War. Lady Lillian served in the Ulster Volunteer Nursing Corps during the war, and was an organiser of the Ulster Division Comforts Fund.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
30 June My P R Only a scrap with my folks letter
as I am soon bound for my rabbit â
protected hole where I intend to pass the
night & to remain part of tomorrow. It is uncertain where I shall be tomorrow
evening but I am fairly certain not to
be able to write for a couple of days now It was a great joy to get 4 letters
of course rather a disappointment that
there were pink paw marks! I think of you now
poor p r with Mr V & do hope you
won't get a fearful head before you move
nor be tired out by the move. I agree that
your partner is having a very easy time in
the move. I hope she doesn't let you be the maid
of all work. Most kind of Uncle Gay give him
my warm thanks. Terrific glaring almost
hols for the pit mates ! All the same when
the p r is taking a little relaxation the b w wishes
her to do it comfortably. Great growls at next
page in this letter. The p r 's white imagination
would have pink wolf pawmarks about if it
were possible. You may well blush at the
censor reading this but I am not responsible
What a pity Mr Bain doesn't now censor
your letters. All kinds of optimistic reports about
including that Germany has offered Alsace
Lorraine & an indemnity to France! Next
she will be offering Hamburg to England in a
few days or something equally wild if we
have any success at all. I don't like optimistism
now. Your b w
W B Spender
